audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed as Job's reply to Eliphaz: weight of grief, arrows of Shaddai, request for death, failed friendship as dry wadis, demand to teach, and insistence that anguish does not equal falsehood.

verses.restored: Job answered and said:
If only my vexation were weighed, and my calamity lifted together on scales!
For now it would be heavier than the sand of the seas; therefore my words have been rash.
For the arrows of Shaddai are in me; my spirit drinks their poison. The terrors of the Divine are arrayed against me.
Does the wild donkey bray over grass, or the ox low over its fodder?
Can tasteless food be eaten without salt, or is there taste in the slime of purslane?
My soul refuses to touch them; they are like loathsome food to me.
If only my request would come, and the Divine would grant my hope:
that the Divine would be willing to crush me, loosen his hand, and cut me off.
Then it would still be my comfort; I would exult in pain that does not spare, for I have not denied the words of the Holy One.
What is my strength, that I should wait? What is my end, that I should prolong my life?
Is my strength the strength of stones, or is my flesh bronze?
Is there any help in me, when sound wisdom has been driven from me?
To one who is fainting, covenant-kindness is owed by his friend, even if he forsakes the fear of Shaddai.
My brothers have betrayed like a wadi, like channels of wadis that pass away,
dark from ice, where snow hides itself;
when they thaw, they vanish; when heat comes, they disappear from their place.
The paths of their way turn aside; they go up into emptiness and perish.
The caravans of Tema looked; the travelers of Sheba hoped for them.
They were ashamed because they had trusted; they came there and were humiliated.
For now you have become nothing; you see terror and are afraid.
Did I say, Give to me, or from your wealth offer a bribe for me?
Or, Deliver me from the hand of an adversary, or ransom me from the hand of ruthless ones?
Teach me, and I myself will be silent; make me understand how I have strayed.
How forceful are upright words! But what does your arguing prove?
Do you intend to reprove words, when the speeches of one in despair are wind?
You would even cast lots over the orphan and bargain over your friend.
Now please turn toward me; surely I will not lie to your face.
Turn now; let there be no injustice. Turn again; my righteousness is still in it.
Is there injustice on my tongue? Can my palate not discern calamities?
